{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Executive",
  "description": "Filmhouse Cinemas Limited is a dynamic film exhibition company. Our expertise are in the areas of: Cinema operations, management of sites, Ownership of sites, Consultancy for cinema development, Design Fit-out; Cinema project management, Film Distribution: Rights Acquisition, Booking into cinemas, and content development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Executive

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Job type: Full time

Job Description


	Keep accurate records for all daily transactions
	Prepare balance sheets
	Process invoices
	Record accounts payable and accounts receivable
	Update internal systems with financial data
	Prepare monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports
	Reconcile bank statements
	Participate in financial audits
	Track bank deposits and payments
	Assist with budget preparation
	Review and implement financial policies
	Manage the fixed assets register and ensuring all additions, reclassifications and disposals are properly recorded


Qualifications


	Candidates should possess a B.SC / HND with a minimum of 3 Years of experience.
	Must have possess ICAN certificate.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-28",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-29",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Filmhouse Cinemas"
    
  },

Finance Executive Job at Filmhouse Cinemas Limited




        
    

        
        Filmhouse Cinemas Limited is a dynamic film exhibition company. Our expertise are in the areas of: Cinema operations, management of sites, Ownership of sites, Consultancy for cinema development, Design Fit-out; Cinema project management, Film Distribution: Rights Acquisition, Booking into cinemas, and content development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Executive

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Job type: Full time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Keep accurate records for all daily transactions
	Prepare balance sheets
	Process invoices
	Record accounts payable and accounts receivable
	Update internal systems with financial data
	Prepare monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports
	Reconcile bank statements
	Participate in financial audits
	Track bank deposits and payments
	Assist with budget preparation
	Review and implement financial policies
	Manage the fixed assets register and ensuring all additions, reclassifications and disposals are properly recorded


Qualifications


	Candidates should possess a B.SC / HND with a minimum of 3 Years of experience.
	Must have possess ICAN certificate.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Financial Control & Compliance (FC&C) Lead",
  "description": "PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world&#39;s best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Control &amp; Compliance (FC&amp;C) Lead

Job ID: JR002790
Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos State

Key Accountabilities / Responsibilities


	Influence, communicate and liaise with all levels of the business, including management, ALT and Board level as required.
	Be a subject matter expert in the key risks and controls across PZC finance and Entity Level Controls &ndash; and wider across the group, as required.
	Create and develop relationships and networks across the whole of PZC, which will inform and create opportunities to cross-skill and share information, working in partnership, improving risk and controls understanding, awareness of the three lines of defense and business performance.
	Proactively use relationships and networks to identify and analyse opportunities for financial risk and control improvement.
	Liaise with internal and external auditors to share information and address risks / issues identified.
	Oversee the tracking, monitoring and reporting of remediation actions raised by internal and external audit &ndash; working with all business areas as required.
	Proactively analyse and investigate situations identified and create new, unique and pragmatic solutions to address/improve issues identified, including practical implementation of solutions, communication, stakeholder buy in and monitoring.
	Identify, escalate, define and execute special and ad hoc work as required, including leading and taking responsibility for financial internal control projects, and provide SME support for any special investigations and assistance to business areas where required
	Perform regular analytics and challenge status quo in identifying risks and controls across finance and work with the Process Owners to identify and implement financial improvement and/or efficiency improvements for the business as a whole, influencing management to implement the required process and behavioral changes e.g. compliance with travel and expense policies
	Using analytical data, identify areas for further investigation or remedial action, define and action as appropriate.
	Implement, monitor and action as required to ensure &ndash; robust testing and audit practices when performing all audit work.
	Reporting to senior management including status reports and dashboard including identification of themes and trends, creation of resultant actions / consequences.
	Perform testing and audit monitoring / follow-up work as appropriate to evaluate the existence and effectiveness of corrective action (via re-visit or contact with management).
	Identify requirements and monitor and report adherence to external business requirements and legislation Identify requirements and monitor and report adherence to internal policies and procedures across the group.
	Remain current with requirements and proactively identify opportunities to improve existing processes.
	Create new efficient and effective initiatives to address work requirements, e.g. new methods of testing, training or communication


Role Requirements
Knowledge / Qualifications:


	University Degree
	Minimum 5 years of post-qualification experience
	Qualified accountant / Internal or external Auditor
	Proven track record when operating in an ICFR / SOX environment either locally or as part of a multi-national Group.


Technical / Professional Experience:


	Is considered to have foundation competency in Reporting, Performance Management, Analysis &amp; Insight, and Business Understanding.
	Is considered to have fully operational competency in Accounting, Risk Management, Systems &amp; Processes, and Business Impact.
	Is considered to have a mastery competency in Controls.
	FMCG experience preferred
	Experience of performing analytical review and systematically identifying which data to gather, which sources to use, to gather information from a variety of sources, analyse information, identify implications of data, draw appropriate conclusions, generate viable alternative solutions to a question or problem, and evaluate the consequences of choosing each alternative
	Knowledge of and ability to challenge financial processes.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-28",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-05",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "PZ Cussons"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "199607"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Financial Control & Compliance (FC&C) Lead Job at PZ Cussons




        
    

        
        PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world's best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Control & Compliance (FC&C) Lead

Job ID: JR002790
Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos State

Key Accountabilities / Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Influence, communicate and liaise with all levels of the business, including management, ALT and Board level as required.
	Be a subject matter expert in the key risks and controls across PZC finance and Entity Level Controls – and wider across the group, as required.
	Create and develop relationships and networks across the whole of PZC, which will inform and create opportunities to cross-skill and share information, working in partnership, improving risk and controls understanding, awareness of the three lines of defense and business performance.
	Proactively use relationships and networks to identify and analyse opportunities for financial risk and control improvement.
	Liaise with internal and external auditors to share information and address risks / issues identified.
	Oversee the tracking, monitoring and reporting of remediation actions raised by internal and external audit – working with all business areas as required.
	Proactively analyse and investigate situations identified and create new, unique and pragmatic solutions to address/improve issues identified, including practical implementation of solutions, communication, stakeholder buy in and monitoring.
	Identify, escalate, define and execute special and ad hoc work as required, including leading and taking responsibility for financial internal control projects, and provide SME support for any special investigations and assistance to business areas where required
	Perform regular analytics and challenge status quo in identifying risks and controls across finance and work with the Process Owners to identify and implement financial improvement and/or efficiency improvements for the business as a whole, influencing management to implement the required process and behavioral changes e.g. compliance with travel and expense policies
	Using analytical data, identify areas for further investigation or remedial action, define and action as appropriate.
	Implement, monitor and action as required to ensure – robust testing and audit practices when performing all audit work.
	Reporting to senior management including status reports and dashboard including identification of themes and trends, creation of resultant actions / consequences.
	Perform testing and audit monitoring / follow-up work as appropriate to evaluate the existence and effectiveness of corrective action (via re-visit or contact with management).
	Identify requirements and monitor and report adherence to external business requirements and legislation Identify requirements and monitor and report adherence to internal policies and procedures across the group.
	Remain current with requirements and proactively identify opportunities to improve existing processes.
	Create new efficient and effective initiatives to address work requirements, e.g. new methods of testing, training or communication


Role Requirements
Knowledge / Qualifications:


	University Degree
	Minimum 5 years of post-qualification experience
	Qualified accountant / Internal or external Auditor
	Proven track record when operating in an ICFR / SOX environment either locally or as part of a multi-national Group.


Technical / Professional Experience:


	Is considered to have foundation competency in Reporting, Performance Management, Analysis & Insight, and Business Understanding.
	Is considered to have fully operational competency in Accounting, Risk Management, Systems & Processes, and Business Impact.
	Is considered to have a mastery competency in Controls.
	FMCG experience preferred
	Experience of performing analytical review and systematically identifying which data to gather, which sources to use, to gather information from a variety of sources, analyse information, identify implications of data, draw appropriate conclusions, generate viable alternative solutions to a question or problem, and evaluate the consequences of choosing each alternative
	Knowledge of and ability to challenge financial processes.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Care Supervisor

Job ID: JR002717
Job Location: Nigeria

Job Summary


	Our purpose is for everyone, for life, for good.
	Sustainability and the wellbeing of people, families and communities everywhere are at the heart of everything that we do.
	Since our founding in 1884, we have been creating products to delight, care for and nourish consumers.
	Across our core categories of Hygiene, Baby and Beauty, our trusted and well-loved brands include Carex, Cussons Baby, Sanctuary Spa and St. Tropez.


Responsibilities


	Answer incoming calls and respond to customer emails.
	Manage and resolve customer complaints effectively.
	Identify issues and escalate them to supervisors when necessary.
	Provide customers with information about products and services.
	Route calls to the appropriate resources as needed.
	Document all call details according to standard operating procedures.
	Follow up on customer calls when required.
	Complete call logs and reports accurately.
	Perform other assigned duties as needed.
	Ensure efficient use of resources and minimize environmental pollution while carrying out responsibilities.
	Maintain effective safety management practices.
	Sustain quality systems management.
	Contribute to the implementation of sustainable continuous improvement systems


Requirements
Qualifications:


	First Degree in any discipline
	Computer Literacy


Experiences: (What experience brings success to this role):


	1-2 years of experience in customer service , and over this period has successfully delivered on KPIs.
	Knowledge of customer service practices and principles.
	Excellent data entry and typing skills.
	Excellent listening skills, along with strong verbal and written communication abilities. communication skills.
	Ability to handle stressful situations appropriately.

Customer Care Supervisor Job at PZ Cussons




        
    

        
        PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world's best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Care Supervisor

Job ID: JR002717
Job Location: Nigeria

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our purpose is for everyone, for life, for good.
	Sustainability and the wellbeing of people, families and communities everywhere are at the heart of everything that we do.
	Since our founding in 1884, we have been creating products to delight, care for and nourish consumers.
	Across our core categories of Hygiene, Baby and Beauty, our trusted and well-loved brands include Carex, Cussons Baby, Sanctuary Spa and St. Tropez.


Responsibilities


	Answer incoming calls and respond to customer emails.
	Manage and resolve customer complaints effectively.
	Identify issues and escalate them to supervisors when necessary.
	Provide customers with information about products and services.
	Route calls to the appropriate resources as needed.
	Document all call details according to standard operating procedures.
	Follow up on customer calls when required.
	Complete call logs and reports accurately.
	Perform other assigned duties as needed.
	Ensure efficient use of resources and minimize environmental pollution while carrying out responsibilities.
	Maintain effective safety management practices.
	Sustain quality systems management.
	Contribute to the implementation of sustainable continuous improvement systems


Requirements
Qualifications:


	First Degree in any discipline
	Computer Literacy


Experiences: (What experience brings success to this role):


	1-2 years of experience in customer service , and over this period has successfully delivered on KPIs.
	Knowledge of customer service practices and principles.
	Excellent data entry and typing skills.
	Excellent listening skills, along with strong verbal and written communication abilities. communication skills.
	Ability to handle stressful situations appropriately.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Online Sales Agent",
  "description": "Marvel Advisory is a recruitment agency that was founded in 2018 by a team of Human Resources and Business Consultants with the aim of transforming the workforce by inspiring productivity, effectiveness, value driven and innovative teams across businesses in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Online Sales Agent

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a smart, customer-focused, and proactive Online Sales Personnel to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing online customer interactions, responding to inquiries promptly, and driving sales through our social media platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Attend to customers and sales inquiries across all social media platforms
	Respond promptly and professionally to messages, comments, and online requests
	Convert inquiries into sales and maintain positive customer relationships
	Follow up with customers on orders, payments, and deliveries
	Provide accurate information about products, pricing, and promotions
	Maintain proper records of customer interactions and sales activities
	Collaborate with the team to improve customer experience and sales performance
	Learn and understand company products and operational processes thoroughly.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification
	Previous experience in online sales, customer service, or social media management is an added advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Good typing and phone communication skills
	Must be social media savvy and comfortable using WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and other online platforms
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and multitask effectively
	Must be reliable, responsive, and customer-oriented.


Work Schedule:


	Physical office hours: Monday &ndash; Saturday, 8:30 AM &ndash; 5:30 PM
	Online customer support coverage: 6:00 AM &ndash; 10:00 PM (rotational schedule applies after training and mastery of company processes/products).


Preferred Location:


	Applicants residing in or around the following areas will be strongly preferred: Ajao Estate, Isolo, Osolo, Oshodi, Mafoluku, Ejigbo, Ikotun, Mushin, Oke Afa.


Salary


	N100,000 - N120,000 monthly (subject to increase after confirmation)
	Annual leave after confirmation
	Opportunity for growth and flexible rotational scheduling after training.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: marvelrecruitment7@gmail.com using &quot;Online Sales Agents&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  },

Online Sales Agent Job at Marvel Advisory




        
    

        
        Marvel Advisory is a recruitment agency that was founded in 2018 by a team of Human Resources and Business Consultants with the aim of transforming the workforce by inspiring productivity, effectiveness, value driven and innovative teams across businesses in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Online Sales Agent

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a smart, customer-focused, and proactive Online Sales Personnel to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing online customer interactions, responding to inquiries promptly, and driving sales through our social media platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Attend to customers and sales inquiries across all social media platforms
	Respond promptly and professionally to messages, comments, and online requests
	Convert inquiries into sales and maintain positive customer relationships
	Follow up with customers on orders, payments, and deliveries
	Provide accurate information about products, pricing, and promotions
	Maintain proper records of customer interactions and sales activities
	Collaborate with the team to improve customer experience and sales performance
	Learn and understand company products and operational processes thoroughly.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification
	Previous experience in online sales, customer service, or social media management is an added advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Good typing and phone communication skills
	Must be social media savvy and comfortable using WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and other online platforms
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and multitask effectively
	Must be reliable, responsive, and customer-oriented.


Work Schedule:


	Physical office hours: Monday – Saturday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM
	Online customer support coverage: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (rotational schedule applies after training and mastery of company processes/products).


Preferred Location:


	Applicants residing in or around the following areas will be strongly preferred: Ajao Estate, Isolo, Osolo, Oshodi, Mafoluku, Ejigbo, Ikotun, Mushin, Oke Afa.


Salary


	N100,000 - N120,000 monthly (subject to increase after confirmation)
	Annual leave after confirmation
	Opportunity for growth and flexible rotational scheduling after training.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Online Sales Agents" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Industry: Retail / Home &amp; Lifestyle

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly experienced and detail-oriented Accountant to oversee the company&rsquo;s financial operations, statutory compliance, payroll management, financial reporting, and strategic financial planning across multiple locations.
	The ideal candidate will play a key role in ensuring financial accuracy, operational efficiency, compliance with Nigerian financial regulations, and providing management with timely financial insights to support business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily financial operations and transaction processing across all business locations.
	Prepare financial reports, management accounts, profit &amp; loss statements, and cash flow reports.
	Reconcile bank accounts, invoices, petty cash, and financial records.
	Oversee payroll processing, PAYE, pension remittance, NHF, HMO reconciliations, and statutory compliance.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian financial regulations and tax laws.
	Develop and monitor budgets, forecasts, and financial performance metrics.
	Prepare weekly and monthly reconciliation reports.
	Manage accounts receivable and payable processes.
	Liaise with banks, auditors, regulatory bodies, and external stakeholders.
	Support annual audit processes and statutory reporting.
	Supervise and provide guidance to the Accounts &amp; Administration Officer.
	Provide financial analysis and strategic recommendations to management.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field with 3&ndash;5 years of progressive accounting experience.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent is required.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax laws, payroll administration, and statutory compliance.
	Proficiency in Sage Accounting Software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent analytical, reporting, and reconciliation skills.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple deadlines.
	Experience in retail, consumer goods, or multi-branch operations is an added advantage.


Preferred Skills:


	Financial reporting &amp; management accounting
	Payroll and statutory deductions management
	Budgeting and variance analysis
	Petty cash and expense oversight
	Audit support and compliance management
	Strong communication and leadership skills


Salary and Benefits


	N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.
	Opportunity to work with a growing multi-location retail brand
	Collaborative and professional work environment
	Strategic leadership exposure
	Career growth and development opportunities


Method of Application

Accountant Job at Marvel Advisory




        
    

        
        Marvel Advisory is a recruitment agency that was founded in 2018 by a team of Human Resources and Business Consultants with the aim of transforming the workforce by inspiring productivity, effectiveness, value driven and innovative teams across businesses in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos (Oversight across Ikoyi, Victoria Island, Lekki, Surulere & Abuja branches)
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Schedule: Monday – Saturday
Industry: Retail / Home & Lifestyle

About the Role

	We are seeking a highly experienced and detail-oriented Accountant to oversee the company’s financial operations, statutory compliance, payroll management, financial reporting, and strategic financial planning across multiple locations.
	The ideal candidate will play a key role in ensuring financial accuracy, operational efficiency, compliance with Nigerian financial regulations, and providing management with timely financial insights to support business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily financial operations and transaction processing across all business locations.
	Prepare financial reports, management accounts, profit & loss statements, and cash flow reports.
	Reconcile bank accounts, invoices, petty cash, and financial records.
	Oversee payroll processing, PAYE, pension remittance, NHF, HMO reconciliations, and statutory compliance.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian financial regulations and tax laws.
	Develop and monitor budgets, forecasts, and financial performance metrics.
	Prepare weekly and monthly reconciliation reports.
	Manage accounts receivable and payable processes.
	Liaise with banks, auditors, regulatory bodies, and external stakeholders.
	Support annual audit processes and statutory reporting.
	Supervise and provide guidance to the Accounts & Administration Officer.
	Provide financial analysis and strategic recommendations to management.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field with 3–5 years of progressive accounting experience.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent is required.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax laws, payroll administration, and statutory compliance.
	Proficiency in Sage Accounting Software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent analytical, reporting, and reconciliation skills.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple deadlines.
	Experience in retail, consumer goods, or multi-branch operations is an added advantage.


Preferred Skills:


	Financial reporting & management accounting
	Payroll and statutory deductions management
	Budgeting and variance analysis
	Petty cash and expense oversight
	Audit support and compliance management
	Strong communication and leadership skills


Salary and Benefits


	N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.
	Opportunity to work with a growing multi-location retail brand
	Collaborative and professional work environment
	Strategic leadership exposure
	Career growth and development opportunities


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Accountant" as the subject of the email.
